summary: The test suite is thorough across its stated layers. Unit tests cover all four parsing paths (package.json happy path, empty, invalid JSON, requirements.txt with comments/blank lines, dispatch, unsupported type, missing file), all four license-classification outcomes (approved, denied, unknown-not-in-list, unknown-None return), deny-wins-over-allow, both report states (compliant/non-compliant), end-to-end run(), and CLI main() with both exit codes. Workflow tests validate actionlint, YAML trigger/job structure, and file-reference existence. Act-driven parametric cases (mixed, all-approved, unknown-only) assert exact RESULT lines and job success. Gaps that limit the score: CLI main() error paths (missing config, bad license-db, bad manifest path) are untested; requirements.txt missing-file raises ManifestError but no dedicated test covers it through parse_manifest; empty allow/deny lists and duplicate names across dependencies/devDependencies aren't exercised; and the _db_lookup helper is only exercised indirectly. Design is clean — mock_lookup factory, tmp_path throughout, parametrize with CASES list, session-scoped autouse fixture for act-result.txt, and clear section comments all reflect good TDD discipline. Minor issue: _have_act_and_docker() is evaluated at collection time rather than inside a fixture, and there is no shared conftest.py to expose mock_lookup to multiple test files. Overall a well-constructed suite that would reliably catch regressions in the core logic; the main gap is incomplete error-path coverage in the CLI layer.
